what: Pre-allocate moe_sorting + intermediate buffers at module scope: cache sorted_ids/sorted_weights/sorted_expert_ids/num_valid_ids/moe_buf/a2 tensors per shape config to avoid torch.empty allocations on each call
why: Each fused_moe call allocates 6+ temporary tensors. PyTorch memory allocation has overhead (~5-10us per call). Pre-allocating and reusing per-shape buffers eliminates this. The LB uses recheck=True so inputs change, but tensor shapes are constant per benchmark shape.

what: Full inline 2-stage pipeline with pre-allocated a2/quant buffers: bypass fused_moe_2stages entirely, directly call quant + stage1 + re-quant + stage2 with all intermediate tensors pre-allocated per shape
why: v165 showed -1.9% from pre-allocating moe_sorting buffers alone. fused_moe_2stages still allocates a2 (intermediate GEMM output) and quant buffers (a1, a1_scale, a2_quant, a2_scale) on each call. Pre-allocating these eliminates ~4 more tensor allocations per call.

what: cktile_moe ksplit=3 for bs=128/E=257/d=256 (instead of ksplit=2)
why: ksplit=2 gave best results for E=257 shapes, ksplit=4 was +0.6% worse. With d_hidden=7168, ksplit=3 is not valid (7168/3 is not divisible by 256). Actually checking: 7168 % 3 = 2, so ksplit=3 won't work. Let me try ksplit=2 with block_m=32 instead of 16.

what: 4-WG M64 stage1 (256x64x128x128_1x4) for bs=128/E=33/d=512 with block_m=64, matching stage1 MPerBlock to sorting block_m
why: Currently using M128 stage1 with block_m=64 sorting. The MPerBlock/block_m mismatch may cause inefficiency: stage1 processes 128-token tiles but sorting groups tokens in 64-token blocks. M64 stage1 kernel processes smaller tiles that exactly match the sorting granularity.
override: v157 tried M64 for d=2048/bs=512 only, this targets d=512/bs=128
